mnemonics.title: Structure Definition Remember
mnemonics.content: STRUCT: S-ingle T-ype R-epresents U-nified C-ombined T-ypes. Think of STRUCTURE as a BLUEPRINT that groups different data types together.

mnemonics.title: Dot vs Arrow Operator
mnemonics.content: Use DOT (.) when you have the actual variable (obj.member). Use ARROW (->) when you have a POINTER to the variable (ptr->member). MEMORIZE: DOT for DIRECT, ARROW for POINTER.

mnemonics.title: Structure vs Array
mnemonics.content: ARRAYS ARE ALIKE (same type), STRUCTURES ARE UNLIKE (different types). A- SAME, S- DIFFERENT.

acronyms.expansion: Structure - A user-defined data type that groups related items of different types
acronyms.usage: Used to define a structure: struct Student { int rollno; };

acronyms.expansion: Type Definition - Creates an alias for existing data types
acronyms.usage: Simplifies structure declarations: typedef struct { } Student; then use Student s1;

visualTips.visualization: Think of a structure as a BUILDING BLUEPRINT. The blueprint (structure definition) describes what a building will have (rooms, floors, windows), but the actual building (structure variable) is the physical instance created from that blueprint.
Visualize structure members as boxes placed side-by-side in memory. Padding is like EMPTY SPACES placed between boxes to align them properly. Total size = sum of boxes + empty spaces.

Imagine a STRUCTURE VARIABLE as a RECORD FORM. The form has fields (members) like Name, Address, Phone - each accepting different types of information. Multiple copies of the same form can be filled (multiple structure variables).

analogies.analogy: ARRAY is like a TRAIN where all cars (elements) are the SAME type (passenger cars). STRUCTURE is like a TRAIN with DIFFERENT car types (engine, passenger car, cargo car, dining car) all coupled together.
STRUCTURE DEFINITION is like a COOKIE CUTTER (the template). STRUCTURE VARIABLE is like the COOKIE made with that cutter. One cutter (definition) can make many cookies (variables).
Structure members are like DIFFERENT ATTRIBUTES of a PERSON: name (string), age (int), height (float), isStudent (boolean). Each attribute has a different type, but they all describe the same person.
